title: Core Accounting Theory for Engineering
subhead: Applying Debits, Credits, and Capex to a Services Firm.

chunks.title: The Accounting Equation
chunks.bodyHtml:
Everything is built on one simple equation: Assets = Liabilities + Equity.

For Skarion Engineering, our biggest Assets aren't factories. They are Cash, Accounts Receivable (money owed to us for fiber designs), and capitalized software.

chunks.title: Categorize the Accounts
chunks.instruction: Drag each account to its correct classification for an OSP Engineering Firm.

chunks.title: Capitalizing vs. Expensing
chunks.bodyHtml:
If we buy a $50 box of pens, we expense it immediately. But if we buy a $15,000 server to host our GIS databases, we capitalize it. This means we record it as an Asset on the Balance Sheet and slowly expense it over its useful life (Depreciation).

Why? Because that server will help us generate revenue for 5 years, so we spread the cost over 5 years.

chunks.title: Record a Software Purchase
chunks.scenarioHtml: Skarion Engineering just purchased an $8,000 annual enterprise license for QGIS and Vetro using our operating checking account. Since it benefits the whole year, we capitalize it as a Prepaid Asset.
